75279.subject: 140-Company Consortium Unveils Open USD
75279.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

* **[Open USD](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/open-standard-open-usd-ousd-stablecoin-consortium-blackrock-visa-mastercard)**[ debuts as a 140-company consortium](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/open-standard-open-usd-ousd-stablecoin-consortium-blackrock-visa-mastercard)

* **[Securitize](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/securitize-shareholders-approve-merger-first-publicly-traded-tokenization-company)**[ clears its merger ](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/securitize-shareholders-approve-merger-first-publicly-traded-tokenization-company)

* **[Magic Eden](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/magic-eden-founders-sued-by-usdme-buyers-over-broken-utility-promises)**[ and its founders are sued ](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/magic-eden-founders-sued-by-usdme-buyers-over-broken-utility-promises)

* **[Lighter](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/lighter-to-burn-repurchased-lit-fund-staking-from-ecosystem-reserve)**[ will burn repurchased LIT and fund staking from its reserve](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/lighter-to-burn-repurchased-lit-fund-staking-from-ecosystem-reserve)

* **[Machi Big Brother](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/machi-big-brother-s-hyperliquid-losses-top-usd80m-as-he-sells-bored-apes-for-margin)**['s Hyperliquid losses top $80M ](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/machi-big-brother-s-hyperliquid-losses-top-usd80m-as-he-sells-bored-apes-for-margin)

* **[Cosmos Labs Acquires Mintscan and Team](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/cosmos-labs-acquires-mintscan-cosmos-korea-expansion)**[ (Partner)](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/cosmos-labs-acquires-mintscan-cosmos-korea-expansion)

* **[Inside DTCC's Move to Stellar](https://thedefiant.io/education/explainers/dtcc-stellar-tokenization-dtc-custodied-assets-explainer)**[ (Partner)](https://thedefiant.io/education/explainers/dtcc-stellar-tokenization-dtc-custodied-assets-explainer)

A 140-company consortium wants to disrupt Circle and Tether’s money printing machine.

**Open Standard**, a group of more than **140 financial and technology firms** whose backers include **Visa**, **Mastercard**, **BlackRock**, and **Coinbase**, [unveiled Open USD, a partner-governed stablecoin](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/open-standard-open-usd-ousd-stablecoin-consortium-blackrock-visa-mastercard) whose reserve earnings and governance go to the businesses that adopt it, spreading economics a lone issuer normally keeps. **OUSD** is slated to launch later in 2026.

The design targets the exact reason banks and fintechs have hesitated to push existing stablecoins: adopting one hands the float and the customer relationship to a competitor. By pooling issuance and returning the yield to distributors, the consortium is betting that shared economics pull adoption that a single issuer's brand cannot. Whether 140 members can agree on governance is a harder test.

**Read more below!**

————————————

75276.subject: 🟪 The useful lives of machines
75276.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/9f199753-ca31-4e3d-9435-5e12896ecff5/BD_NL_Banner_Heading.png?t=1781793903)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/e60d7cde-4250-41f8-9443-28ed51b80560/divider_transparent.png?t=1774649182)
Caption: 

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3bd8a492-ff61-41db-a7f2-c821d481fd72/BD_NL_QuoteCircle.png?t=1781794162)
Caption: 

“It is an argument for a society’s collective responsibility to respond to decline and decay.”
_— __Dan Bouk on depreciation accounting_



----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/2fb4fbab-11f9-409d-a18a-3cf4fb25ce93/image.png?t=1782855848)
Caption: 

# The useful lives of machines

Dan Bouk [writes](https://asteriskmag.com/issues/14/rust-in-numbers?utm_source=chatgpt.com) a short history of the most powerful idea in accounting: depreciation.

The historian of “things clouded in shrouds of boringness” explains that the bookkeeping convention was first conceived as a kind of actuarial table for machines.

In 1905, he says, a German official was among the first to apply the idea of a “survival curve” to industrial goods — estimating how long wooden telegraph poles were likely to remain useful in the same way insurance companies estimated how long their policyholders were likely to remain alive. 

The practice was slow to catch on, probably because accountants had so little data to work with. Railroad companies, for example, couldn’t possibly keep track of how long their millions of railroad ties remained useful (especially as they were made of all different types of wood and used in all different climates).

Still, companies were at least starting to think about it. Bouk quotes a railroad engineer warning that even perfectly functional equipment “is burning up as surely as is the coal which is shoveled under boilers.” 

Soon, everyone was forced to think harder. In 1909, the Supreme Court ruled that public utilities had to be guaranteed a profit, and that the profit had to account for depreciation.

“Before coming to the question of profit at all,” the majority opinion in [Knoxville v. Knoxville Water Company](https://supreme.justia.com/cases/federal/us/212/1/) read, “the company is entitled to earn enough to provide not only for current repairs, but for making good the depreciation of plant and replacing deteriorated portions thereof."

In other words, the cost of water in Knoxville had to be high enough to account for the finite lifespan of the infrastructure required to provide it. 

Bouk believes the idea had significance far beyond Tennessee: “It is powerful and important that

Good evening, Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner says billionaires should not buy elections, but his campaign filings show that at least five found the donate button anyway. His candidacy has not been without controversy, like a cheating scandal and a tattoo that resembles a Nazi emblem. Nevertheless, more billionaire money is likely to flow into Platner’s coffers as November’s election approaches. Here are the billionaires currently backing his campaign. Thanks for reading! Kris Kornbluth Assistant Editor, Platforms & Curation More Recommended Reads KC Current The KC Current Are Ready To Expand Their Pioneering Soccer Stadium The first venue built for a pro women’s sports team has been a game-changer, and revenue could spike again with the addition of 6,500 seats. America's Best Brands For Value 2026 Which products provide the highest quality for the price? Forbes’ second-annual Best Brands for Value list explores this through a year-long survey that gathered 14 million consumer ratings of more than 2,000 brands.

75274.subject: 🔷 The Daily: Visa, Stripe, Mastercard, Coinbase, BlackRock back OUSD stablecoin
75274.body:
New York Life launches tokenized fund | SEC seeks input on 'novel ETF' | analysts reevaluate Strategy | Zcash nonprofit unveils wallet recovery tool | and more

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by Daniel Kuhn | Edited by Jason Shubnell

Presented by

BitGo ( https://www.bitgo.com/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Tuesday! *Bitcoin* is once again trading below $60,000 as the month comes to a close, driven in part by June's net ETF outflows — the worst month on record since the spot bitcoin funds went live in 2024.

In today's newsletter, *Open Standard* stablecoin initiative launches with support from 140 orgs, *New York Life* gets into tokenization, *SEC* seeks public comment on “novel ETFs,” and more.

Meanwhile, *Sovright* , the new *Zcash* non-profit formed out of the ECC governance dispute, has unveiled a ZEC wallet recovery tool ( https://www.theblock.co/post/406731/new-zcash-nonprofit-sovright-unveils-zec-wallet-recovery-tool?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ).

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*🌐 Visa, Stripe, Coinbase and more join Open USD stablecoin that shares reserve revenue*

CZ ( https://www.theblock.co/post/406736/visa-stripe-coinbase-join-open-usd-stablecoin-shares-reserve-revenue?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Over 140 companies, including *Visa, Stripe, Mastercard, Coinbase, BlackRock, American Express, Google, Shopify, IBM, Ripple* , and others, have joined *Open Standard* to launch *Open USD* (OUSD), a new shared stablecoin expected to launch later in 2026.

* Unlike traditional single-issuer stablecoins, OUSD will be managed by an independent organization with shared governance and revenue-sharing among partner companies.
* OUSD will also distribute most earnings from its reserves to participating businesses, minus a small management fee, allowing members to earn revenue based on the stablecoin’s adoption and usage.
* Businesses can mint and redeem OUSD without fees or volume limits, positioning it as an attractive, neutral payment asset for global transactions.
* Tempo CEO Matt Huang said Open USD will be "natively issued on its network from day one," though it is as yet unclear whether the stablecoin will exclusively launch on *Tempo*.
* While there have been other collaborative stablecoin initiatives in the past, like the Glo
